name: French Fried Onion Rings
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine the flour, meal, beer, and salt using a wire whisk until well mixed.
2. Allow the batter to set at room temperature for 3 to 3 1/2 hours.
3. Preheat the oven to 200°F (95°C) about 30 minutes before frying.
4. Slice the onions into rings that are approximately 1/4 inch thick.
5. Heat the oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to the appropriate temperature for frying.
6. Dip the onion rings into the batter, ensuring they are fully coated.
7. Fry the onion rings until they are brown and crispy.
8. Transfer the fried onion rings to a casserole dish (do not stack them).
9. Keep the onion rings warm in the oven until they reach the desired crispiness.
10. This recipe freezes well and serves 3 people.
Date: Aug. 8, 1997
